content:
Hey all, so I’m looking to start a new routine. I’m a 27 year old male, and weigh around 230lbs with around 30% BF. But first, some background information. My goal is to lose weight and just become all around more physically fit, mobile, and agile. I am more concerned with being able to use my body effectively then I am with trying to Squat 400+ lbs. I would like to be able to do unassisted pull ups, handstands, and the like.

For the past 9 months I’ve been doing Stonglifts 5x5. My lifts are currently:
Squat 260lbs
OHP 100lbs
DL 200lbs
Bench 145lbs
Rows 125lbs

My numbers could be higher, but since I do sometimes struggle getting to the gym 3x per week, the numbers tend to stall a bit.

Before Stronglifts I was doing a combination of different workouts, switching between different Beachbody programs for ~5 years, sometimes focusing on more weight lifting routines, sometimes focusing more on cardio. BB doesn’t really teach you nutrition effectively so while I was getting stronger and more fit, I was still yo-yoing in weight (lowest i weighed was 200lbs). I felt the best I ever felt while doing the BB programs due to their mixed efforts of promoting a total body fitness, but I wanted to put on more muscle. Stronglifts has definitely been helping me get stronger by alot, but it has been causing me some lower back issues as well as some neck issues, and I’m sure I can chalk this up to form but I’m frankly getting sick of doing the same few movements in the same setting time and time again.
